data.content:
The build confirms: the file is generated (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but not compiled. The problem is that **the target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. SDK-style implicit globs expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

The fix: add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up by the implicit `Compile` glob.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after the file is created. Also, it should be added to `FileWrites` so the `Clean` target knows about it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis and fix:

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. SDK-style implicit globs (`**/*.cs`) expand at **evaluation time** — before any targets run — so the file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the generated file is included in compilation and properly cleaned.
